payloads.description: Exact formal-reconstruction status payload mirrored from aisknab/pnp public/pnp-status.json.
Exact compiled Lean declaration inventory mirrored from aisknab/pnp public/pnp-theorem-inventory.json and hash-bound by the current status.
Freeze notice for the former activated verifier-run upload path.
Historical public-review entrypoint and boundary summary.
Historical external-review status record. External review is not a current mathematical premise or blocker.
Frozen historical registry of activated-status verifier records.
Historical policy for comparing normalized fields across old activated-status run records.
Frozen historical comparison matrix. It is not a current theorem-status signal.
Neutral historical matrix summary; no current green badge is published.

nonClaims: The repository does not currently establish P = NP.
The exact current status mirror does not allow public theorem emission.
The pinned Lean PNP library root compiles, but no PNP.Main.p_eq_np theorem exists and four project-specific axioms remain.
PNP.Concrete.FinalUniversalDesign.cnfSATInNP proves concrete CNF-SAT membership in NP only; CNF-SAT in P, NP-completeness, and P = NP remain unproved.

PipelineStageBridges proves collision-free framer-to-simulator and accept/reject-to-handoff launches, exact cumulative work and compiled raw traces, and bounded verdict preservation only for supplied exact target traces.

The local handoff-to-terminal suffix launches accepting and rejecting packer copies, preserves distinct terminal verdict states, and has outputBits equality with compiled bound 18*m^2 + 36*m + 12; its 59 reviewed declarations have empty axiom closure.

For every proof-bearing polynomial-time target and every raw BitString input, one literal raw pipeline preserves the exact verdict and machineOutput, cannot time out, and is bounded by explicit external output and runtime polynomials; all 29 reviewed compiler declarations have empty axiom closure.

The literal finite input framer accepts every raw bitstring, including empty and odd-length inputs, reaches an exact represented endpoint, cannot time out within 6*m^2 + 39*m + 75 raw steps, and has an empty audited axiom closure across 70 declarations.

Collision-free sequential namespaces and a literal sequential compiler preserve exact verdict, machineOutput, no-timeout, acceptance, and stuck-first timeout with Rseq(m) = PipelineRaw(p)(m) + 6 + PipelineRaw(q)(m + p(m) + 1).

FunctionProgram and DecisionProgram raw refinements now compose recursively into one literal finite machine, and a proof-bearing PolynomialTimeDecider compiles to that machine with acceptance equivalence. This discharges the concrete complexity machine link, but CNF-SAT in P, NP-completeness, and P = NP remain unproved.

CookLevinFormulaSize proves an external bound for the actual canonical encoded formula in both verifier input modes. It does not implement or time a raw finite formula builder, package a PolynomialReduction, establish CNF-SAT NP-completeness, establish CNF-SAT in P, or prove P = NP.

CookLevinFormulaSchedule provides an answer-independent rectangular constraint/clause/token/bit schedule with exact external-input polynomial length and exact canonical emission. It is a pure schedule specification: it does not interpret slots as constant-time raw actions, implement or time a raw formula builder, construct a FunctionProgram.RawRefinement, package a PolynomialReduction, establish CNF-SAT NP-completeness, establish CNF-SAT in P, or prove P = NP.

CookLevinFormulaCursor provides direct constraint, clause, token, and raw-bit coordinate decoders with distinct out-of-range, valid-padding, and populated states, plus exact prefix, full, one-step-short, terminal, and excess-fuel traversal whose populated output is the canonical encoded formula. It remains a Lean specification cursor: it does not prove constant-time raw slot interpretation, implement or time a raw finite formula builder, construct a FunctionProgram.RawRefinement, package a PolynomialReduction, establish CNF-SAT NP-completeness, establish CNF-SAT in P, or prove P = NP.

CookLevin.BuilderInputLength is a fixed 19-rule work machine connected definitionally to the all-input framer endpoint. It preserves every source bit and appends an exact unary input-length tally in 2*n*n + 4*n + 2 work steps and 12*n*n + 24*n + 12 compiled raw steps; malformed internal scan symbols and one-step-short fuel time out. This is only input-length preparation: it does not emit formula bits, interpret direct cursor slots as raw transitions, compose a complete raw formula builder, construct a FunctionProgram.RawRefinement, package a concrete PolynomialReduction, establish CNF-SAT NP-completeness, establish CNF-SAT in P, or prove P = NP.

CookLevin.BuilderInputPrefix composes the total all-input framer, one explicit nine-symbol launch, and the fixed unary tally in one collision-free finite work machine. Every raw bitstring reaches the exact preserved-input tally endpoint within 18*n*n + 63*n + 93 compiled raw steps; the unused zeroOne tally-scan symbol and one-step-short fuel remain fail-closed. This is only an executable input-preparation prefix: it emits no formula bit, performs no raw formula-cursor interpretation, supplies no complete builder or construction-time RawRefinement, packages no concrete PolynomialReduction, and establishes neither CNF-SAT NP-completeness, CNF-SAT in P, nor P = NP.

CookLevin.BuilderTokenAppender is a standalone fixed 59-rule machine that appends any selected canonical two-bit CNF token exactly. Its distinguished first-header run emits the first two encoded-formula bits within 24*n + 48 compiled raw steps; malformed tally/output phases and one-step-short fuel time out. BuilderFirstTokenPrefix now composes that table with BuilderInputPrefix and emits the first T token exactly, but it does not produce the remaining width header or formula, and it establishes neither a complete builder RawRefinement, a concrete PolynomialReduction, CNF-SAT NP-completeness, CNF-SAT in P, nor P = NP.
The axiom-free exact-width syntactic NAND enumerator is intentionally noncanonical and may contain duplicates.
Executable truth-table equivalence and the exhaustive reference minimum apply only to finite Boolean direct-wire semantics with an empty profile; no polynomial runtime is proved.
Replacement and slack are proved only for concrete serial framed contexts, not arbitrary support profiles or the locked-NAND family.

Typed local locked-NAND candidates, source-derived accounting, and five exact local square minima do not establish global cross-instance BaselineDistinct, the locked builder or threshold, residual slack at most four, or polynomial runtime.
The report threshold word retains its ordered baseline coordinates plus one final coordinate; the legacy single-output seed is not the report construction.
The legacy synthetic m=2 fixture is quarantined: honest baseline/displayed counts are 86/90, metadata-consistent counts are 95/99, and stored hybrid counts are 91/95.
The proof-bearing conditional threshold boundary derives results from six typed semantic premises but does not instantiate them for source circuits or prove the report theorem.
Its residual-slack-at-most-four result is conditional, not an unconditional theorem for the report locked-NAND family.

Global carrier layout, cross-instance BaselineDistinct, TraceEquivalence, derived final-output laws, answer-independent polynomial construction, the builder, and the report threshold remain incomplete.
The executable residual-route scanner can prove a strict equivalent gain and residual-slack descent only inside the explicit finite implementation list supplied by its caller.
An unresolved explicit-list scan excludes no unlisted gain and does not imply global minimality or ZeroSlack; the empty-list regression remains unresolved with residual slack one.
Exact-minimum and ZeroSlack route outcomes require Lean proofs of semantic minimality and cannot be manufactured by the executable gain scanner.
Candidate-list and global-gain completeness, the ZeroSlack contradiction and completeness theorem, PCCMin loop exactness, residual-band minimization, and polynomial runtime remain incomplete.
Legacy checker acceptance and activated run records are historical assertion-checker evidence only, not mathematical proof.
External review is optional audit evidence and is not a mathematical premise or release blocker.
Historical report, registry, matrix, badge, and activation coordinates are not active theorem-emission surfaces.
